_doc: Single source of truth for phase progress. Hand-edited as work completes. /status reads this. Bump fields honestly — surgical cuts come from honest tracking.

phases.name: Foundation
phases.exit_criterion: sudo reboot recovers everything in <5 min, all health checks green, frontend reachable at domain
phases.exit_criterion_met: Verified 2026-05-23 12:43–12:47 EDT on the post-cold-boot recovery leg of the May-21 reboot drill. All 10 technical checks green within ~3 min of WSL2 terminal open; external probe of https://api.thebullpen.net/actuator/health returned HTTP/2 200 via Cloudflare Tunnel; frontend reachable at https://thebullpen.net. Restore drill PASS in 3 sec end-to-end at 13:02 EDT after the in-container fix (see docs/drills/2026-05-23_restore.md). Critical pre-existing bug surfaced + fixed in same session: snapshot script was producing metadata-only backups — patched infra/backup/clickhouse-snapshot.sh to run clickhouse-backup inside the docker container.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: WSL2 setup (systemd, memory cap, CUDA passthrough verified)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Verified 2026-05-21 on DESKTOP-N30E7RA. kernel 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2; systemd 259 (259.5-0ubuntu3) running; 15 GiB RAM + 4 GiB swap (no /mnt/c/Users/*/.wslconfig — using WSL2 default, which on a ~32 GiB host gives WSL2 ~50%; tighten via .wslconfig if Windows starts swapping); CUDA passthrough OK (NVIDIA driver 591.86, CUDA 13.1, nvidia-smi sees GPU at /usr/lib/wsl/lib/nvidia-smi); Docker 29.1.3 with alepic in docker group; Java 21.0.11-ea at /usr/bin/java.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Domain + Cloudflare DNS + Cloudflare Tunnel pointed at hello-world
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Wired through end-to-end as of 2026-05-21 (Phase 0 Group B). Cloudflare DNS holds the thebullpen.net zone (apex on Vercel, api.thebullpen.net CNAME → tunnel). Tunnel 'bullpen-prod' (id e79da4e2-bda1-4c1d-91a8-6b5b146c1ced) installed as cloudflared.service via `cloudflared service install <TOKEN>`, 4 active edge connections (iad/bos), Public Hostname route api.thebullpen.net → http://localhost:8080. External probe: HTTP/2 200 from https://api.thebullpen.net/actuator/health with our x-correlation-id header proving end-to-end through the tunnel to the Spring backend. Note: tunnel token leaked once in this session's transcript via unmasked systemctl status output, then rotated 2026-05-21 21:05 EDT; old token revoked. Stale May-19 tunnel id 9264c69d-...json files removed from ~/.cloudflared/ + old tunnel deleted from Cloudflare dashboard.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Spring Boot skeleton with api + worker profiles
exit_criteria_checklist.details: backend/ scaffolded 2026-05-19 — Gradle Kotlin DSL, Spring Boot 3.5.4, virtual threads on, both profiles compile and boot. application-api.yml binds 8080, application-worker.yml binds 8081.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Both as systemd services with /health endpoints
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Units installed + enabled + actively serving as of 2026-05-21. bullpen-api (PID 1091, since 18:40 EDT) + bullpen-worker (PID 1092) running as the dedicated `bullpen` system user (uid 997) from /opt/bullpen/app.jar, per the 2026-05-19 phase-0 brief convention. Originally May-19 session brought up these services; my 19:49 session overwrote the unit files with a (mistakenly chosen) alepic-user + /opt/thebullpen variant — corrected at 21:18 EDT to align with the running reality (bullpen-user + /opt/bullpen), units re-enabled. External probe: HTTP/2 200 from https://api.thebullpen.net/actuator/health (via Cloudflare Tunnel — see B2 work). Local: localhost:8080/actuator/health returns {"status":"UP"}. Both units survive reboot (enabled symlinks present in multi-user.target.wants).

exit_criteria_checklist.label: ClickHouse via Docker, accessible from Spring
exit_criteria_checklist.details: infra/docker-compose.yml brings ClickHouse 24.12 up; verified reachable at localhost:8123 with default/thebullpen. Spring data-layer wiring lands in Phase 1 with the first ClickHouse repository.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: SQLite + Flyway running
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Flyway runs V1__init_registry.sql on boot; verified registry.sqlite created on first run.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: React + Vite skeleton, deployed to Vercel, calling Spring /health via CORS
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Live at https://thebullpen.net and https://www.thebullpen.net (both 200, served by Vercel, gray-clouded). Vite + React 19 + Mantine 9 + TanStack Query 5 + Tailwind 4. Vercel Authentication disabled, COMMIT_AUTHOR_REQUIRED resolved (commit author switched to am.picard03@gmail.com). Production deploy on commit f65ce5e. /health CORS preflight verified locally — once api.thebullpen.net Tunnel exists (Phase 0 WSL2 item), the live frontend will hit live backend.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: GitHub repo, Actions running tests on push, deploy.sh working
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Repo live at https://github.com/Alexm-picard/the-bullpen. All three workflows (backend / frontend / training) green on main as of commit 70a916c. deploy.sh rewritten 2026-05-21 from Phase-0 stub to a real local-WSL2 deploy: clean-tree guard, bootJar build, atomic symlink swap at /opt/bullpen/app.jar, systemctl restart + 30s health-poll smoke, rollback on smoke failure, releases-dir retention (last 5), tag + docs/deploys/<TAG>.md record. First real run gated on /deploy-safely + the live-game window (decision [21]). NOTE: corrected install paths from /opt/thebullpen → /opt/bullpen and User=alepic → User=bullpen at 21:18 EDT to match the May-19 phase-0 brief's system-user convention; aligns with the JAR currently running at /opt/bullpen/app.jar.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Prometheus + Grafana with one trivial dashboard
exit_criteria_checklist.details: infra/docker-compose.yml brings Prometheus 3.0 + Grafana 11.4 up with provisioned datasource and a JVM-overview dashboard. Prometheus scrapes the Spring actuator endpoint via host.docker.internal.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: External uptime monitor /health (Uptime Robot)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: External uptime monitor pointed at https://api.thebullpen.net/health (the custom HealthController endpoint — returns 200 with body {"profile":"api","ts":"...","status":"ok"}). Currently green. Alert channel: Discord (via the monitor's webhook alert contact; separate from the BULLPEN_DISCORD_WEBHOOK that clickhouse-snapshot.sh uses for backup-failure pings). Original setup 2026-05-21 used Better Stack (Phase-0 Group B); vendor switched to Uptime Robot 2026-05-23 per decision [129] — same endpoint, same Discord channel, check interval shifts from 30s (Better Stack free tier) to 5 min (Uptime Robot free tier). Better Stack monitor remains up until the Uptime Robot replacement is wired so external coverage is never dark. NOTE: /actuator/health is also live and returns the standard Spring Actuator body; deploy.sh smoke-checks against /actuator/health while the external monitor hits the lighter /health endpoint.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Backup of empty ClickHouse to USB + restore drill (with empty data)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: PASS 2026-05-23 13:02 EDT. ops/scripts/restore-drill.sh exists + runs end-to-end in ~3 sec (snapshot → spin scratch CH 24.12 on bullpen-drill-net:18123/19000 → restore → row-count + schema parity verify → teardown). docs/drills/2026-05-23_restore.md captures the full report. Critical pre-existing bug surfaced during the drill: snapshot script was producing metadata-only backups (clickhouse-backup running on the host couldn't see FREEZE output in the docker volume's shadow dir). Patched infra/backup/clickhouse-snapshot.sh to run clickhouse-backup inside the bullpen-clickhouse container with a fail-loud sanity check on captured data parts. Layer 2 USB (BULLPEN_BAK, 2026-05-21): operational with first verification backup at 18 MB. Layer 1 systemd timer (bullpen-snapshot.timer): enabled, Persistent=true catch-up verified on 2026-05-23 boot.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: ADR system established (docs/adr/ + first 7 ADRs covering initial tech decisions from design.md §10 + operational-discipline decisions from 2026-05-21)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: ADR-0001–0005 written 2026-05-19 — Java not Kotlin, ONNX in-process not sidecar, ClickHouse + SQLite not Postgres-only, Mantine+Tailwind not pure Tailwind, polling not WebSockets; decisions.md entries [121]–[125] reference them. ADR-0006 (dev/prod boundary — macOS dev only, no edits on the prod box) and ADR-0007 (S3-compatible storage — R2 prod, MinIO offline; revised same day from B2 to R2 per decision [128]) written 2026-05-21; decisions.md entries [126]–[128] reference them. ADR-0006 and ADR-0007 are auto-imported into Claude context via @docs/adr/... in CLAUDE.md because they're easiest to violate silently. Template at docs/adr/TEMPLATE.md.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Dev / prod boundary formalized (ADR-0006) — macOS is the only authoring machine; no editor on the WSL2 working copy; deploy.sh + CI are the only writers
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Rule is in place: ADR-0006 written + auto-imported into CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE.md discipline rule 11 codifies it. Deferred concrete deliverables (Phase 1+, gated on having Mac-side work to drive them): (a) make train-sample target for stratified-sample iteration on the MacBook, (b) make train-full target that ssh-triggers the desktop, (c) bootstrap docs re-verified by a Phase 1 restore drill from a clean WSL2 install. Acknowledged exception on 2026-05-23: the host-side restore drill and the surfaced snapshot-script bugfix required editing on the WSL2 box. The edits were drill-driven and immediately committed to git; not a pattern to continue once feature work resumes on the Mac.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: S3-compatible object storage online (ADR-0007) — Cloudflare R2 in prod (per decision [128]), MinIO offline on the portable drive, S3_ENDPOINT_URL is the only environment-specific knob
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Host-side complete 2026-05-22 + reboot-verified 2026-05-23 (Phase 0 exit): (a) ✓ Cloudflare R2 bucket bullpen-prod created with samples/dev/, snapshots/, raw/ prefixes (.keep markers), token bullpen-prod-rw scoped Object R+W to that single bucket; (b) ✓ rclone remote 'bullpen-r2' configured against the R2 S3-compatible endpoint with no_check_bucket=true, write/read/delete round-trip verified, survives reboot (item 7 of the 2026-05-22 reboot drill returned all 3 prefixes). Deferred to Phase 1+ (gated on a code path that actually needs object storage): (c) boto3 (Python) and aws-sdk-s3 (Java) clients reading S3_ENDPOINT_URL — lands in Phase 2 (sample loading) and Phase 3a (model registration). (d) MinIO launchable from /Volumes/MyDrive/bullpen-data via make minio-up — Mac-side, ships with the first feature that needs offline storage. (e) make sync-mirror running rclone sync from R2 to the portable drive — depends on (d). (f) clickhouse-backup upload to bullpen-r2:bullpen-prod/snapshots/clickhouse/ from clickhouse-snapshot.sh — currently local-only; R2 push lands in Phase 1 (see docs/drills/2026-05-23_restore.md follow-ups). Manifest validation (feature schema hash) lands with the first snapshot-write path in Phase 3a.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Structured JSON logging baseline (logback-json Java side, structlog Python side, correlation IDs propagated through every request)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Java side: logback-spring.xml with logstash-logback-encoder, profile-switched JSON appender, CorrelationIdFilter injects X-Correlation-Id into MDC and echoes the header. Python side: training/src/bullpen_training/logging_config.py with structlog + contextvars-based correlation_id.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Multi-stage Dockerfile for the JAR with non-root appuser (UID 1001) and HEALTHCHECK directive
exit_criteria_checklist.details: backend/Dockerfile: temurin:21-jdk-alpine builder + temurin:21-jre-alpine runtime, appuser UID 1001, tini PID 1, HEALTHCHECK against /actuator/health every 15s.

phases.notes: If >3 weeks, diagnose the blocker. Most likely: WSL/CUDA setup or DNS confusion.

phases.name: Vertical Slice
phases.exit_criterion: Visit thebullpen.net/parks, click a batted ball, see real prediction in <500ms end-to-end
phases.exit_criterion_met: Verified live 2026-05-23 ~22:00 EDT after 3 prod deploys (v2026.05.23-2139 / -2149 / -2156). curl POST https://api.thebullpen.net/v1/predict/batted-ball with the canonical payload returns {"probHr":0.78668094,"modelName":"_toy_batted_ball","modelVersion":"v0","latencyMicros":507,"correlationId":"..."} — well under the 500ms ceiling (507μs inference, ~150ms total round-trip through Cloudflare Tunnel). https://thebullpen.net/parks loads (apex → www 308 → 200) and renders the 10 example BIPs. Three small WSL2 fixes landed in commit dbb9107: deploy.sh systemctl pre-flight pipe was breaking under WSL2 pipefail; ApiErrorAdvice added explicit 404 + 405 handlers (were defaulting to 500); CorsConfig now allows www.thebullpen.net (Vercel serves there).

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Historical Statcast pull (one season ~2024) → raw_statcast
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Implemented under training/src/bullpen_training/ingest/ (statcast_pull.py + clickhouse_client.py + assertions.py + migrations.py). 2024 full season pulled on Mac dev ClickHouse 2026-05-23 18:04 EDT: 760,248 raw rows total / 711,898 regular-season (game_type='R') in 26.5s wall time across 8 monthly chunks. Idempotency verified: re-pull leaves count unchanged via ALTER TABLE DROP PARTITION + re-INSERT. V002__raw_statcast.sql applied via lightweight Python migrations helper (proper Flyway-CH lands in Phase 3). 14 unit tests pass (normalize_columns, month_window, NaN→None coercion, alias mapping). ruff format/lint clean, pyright 0 errors with strict mode on logging_config.py + __init__.py and relaxed for data-handling subpackage.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Cleaning into pitches table with proper schema + dedup
exit_criteria_checklist.details: V003__pitches.sql defines ReplacingMergeTree(ingested_at) keyed on (game_date, game_id, at_bat_index, pitch_number) with Enum8 description and bitmask base_state. Orchestrator at training/src/bullpen_training/ingest/transform_pitches.py runs the SQL transform + 6 assertions (regular-season count band, zero-ID guard, launch_speed/angle range, unknown-description cap, dedup consistency). Live run 2026-05-23 18:12 EDT: 711,898 rows in 0.41s transform + 1.33s OPTIMIZE FINAL; raw → pitches delta = 0; all assertions pass; injected duplicate collapses correctly under FINAL. Fixed three real-data deviations from the leaf plan during the build (launch_speed 40 mph floor too tight, three new pitch-clock-era description codes missing from multiIf, plan referenced wrong source column name for outs).

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Minimal batted-ball model: LightGBM, 5 features, single output
exit_criteria_checklist.details: training/src/bullpen_training/battedball/{features_toy,train_toy}.py. Binary HR vs not-HR, 5 features (launch_speed_mph, launch_angle_deg, release_speed_mph, park_id_encoded, stand_is_left). Live train on 2026-05-23 18:22 EDT: AUC 0.9866 on 24,766-row test split (99,064 train); 1.09s training time. Reproducibility verified — two consecutive runs produce byte-identical model.lgb (sha256 64c04f17…). Required adding ORDER BY to the load SQL to defeat ClickHouse FINAL's unstable row order. Model artifact at training/artifacts/_toy/v0/ (gitignored per CLAUDE.md hard-never rule); metadata.json carries provenance (auc, git_commit, trained_at, seed, model_sha256). Intentionally NOT registered (registry namespace reserved for real models, Phase 2). Tests: 11 new (31 total).

exit_criteria_checklist.label: ONNX export + Java loading + parity test passing
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Python export at training/src/bullpen_training/battedball/{export_toy_onnx,parity_fixture}.py — generates model.onnx + feature_pipeline.json + park_hr_rate.json at opset 15 (onnxmltools cap). Java side at backend/src/main/java/net/thebullpen/baseball/inference/{OnnxModel,FeaturePipeline}.java reads the same JSON contract. Parity tests on both sides pass against the committed 16-row fixture (training/tests/fixtures/parity_toy_001*.json) with max ONNX-vs-LightGBM drift = 5.66e-8 (tolerance 1e-6). Tests self-disable on missing artifacts so a fresh clone has green builds; CI integration to regenerate artifacts before parity-test runs lands in 1.5+ work. Boundary discipline (G4 closed): numerics pass through ONNX; lookups stay in Java/Python preprocessors. One regression caught: Spring Flyway recurses into db/migration/ and tried to apply CH SQL against SQLite — moved CH migrations to db/clickhouse/.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: POST /predict/batted-ball endpoint
exit_criteria_checklist.details: POST /v1/predict/batted-ball live. backend/.../api/PredictBattedBallController + DTOs + ApiErrorAdvice + InferenceMetrics + WarmupReadiness. Live local boot 2026-05-23 14:42 EDT: model ready 8s after JVM start, warm-up 3ms, happy-path curl returns probHr=0.787 in 5ms wall time, validation error returns canonical envelope. 5 MockMvc tests pass (happy path + missing field + bad content-type + switch-hitter rejection + launch-speed out-of-range). Latency histogram + prediction counter wired via Micrometer. G11 closed (warm-up gates readiness probe). Added spring-boot-starter-validation to backend deps. Renamed ApiError.Error → ApiError.Body to avoid java.lang.Error clash (Error Prone caught it).

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Park Explorer page with hardcoded historical BIPs
exit_criteria_checklist.details: frontend/src/pages/parks-page.tsx grids 10 hand-picked 2024 BIPs from frontend/src/data/example-bbips.json. Click a card → TanStack Query mutation calls POST /v1/predict/batted-ball, renders p(HR) + model version + latency. React Router with /, /parks routes; AppShell layout with nav. Vercel SPA rewrite added for deep-link. Vite build clean (358KB / 111KB gz).

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Click → real prediction renders in browser
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Verified end-to-end on Mac dev 2026-05-23: backend on 18080 serves /v1/predict/batted-ball, frontend at 5173 wires the mutation. 4 Vitest tests pass (smoke + 3 predict-API contract: happy 200, 4xx PredictError, non-JSON 5xx fallback). Production reachability via Cloudflare Tunnel + Vercel is operational from Phase 0; first deploy ships with the commit.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Primitive prediction logging to ClickHouse
exit_criteria_checklist.details: AsyncPredictionLogger (bounded ArrayBlockingQueue + 1s flusher on virtual thread) wired into PredictBattedBallController. V004__prediction_log.sql defines MergeTree partitioned by month with 18-month TTL. Live e2e 2026-05-23 14:56 EDT: 5 predictions → 5 rows in prediction_log within 3s with features/prediction JSON intact, latency_ms ≈ 0.09 sub-ms. 4 unit tests pass (drain, overflow-drops, writer-failure non-propagation, no-writer noop mode). New ClickHouseMigrationRunner Java component applies V*.sql files on boot (mirrors the Python migrations.py training uses). G2 partial close; G8 closed.

phases.mvp_cuts: Cannot cut — this phase IS the credibility floor
phases.notes: Demoable spine. From here forward is the real project.

phases.name: The Real Models
phases.exit_criterion: Three models registered, all with eval artifacts, all served via Spring, all with passing leakage tests in CI. ECE < 0.02 on test data per model.
phases.current_leaf: **Phase 2b: 2b.1 + 2b.2 complete; post head v1 persisted.** 2b.1 added Tier 4 features (V008/V009/V010 cascade; folds 3+4 rebuilt with all 4 tiers; 99.5%+ Tier 4 coverage for 2024-25). 2b.2 implemented `bullpen_training.pitch.train_post` (41-feature post head reusing train_pre hyperparameters + isotonic; deterministic alphabetical pitch_type_int mapping) + `--model post` CLI + 6 unit tests. **The earlier 'fold 3+4 hang' was diagnosed as stdout block-buffering of LightGBM's bare `print()` from `log_evaluation` under `... 2>&1 | tee log` (training was completing; logs were never flushed).** Fixed via `lgb.register_logger(logging.getLogger('lightgbm'))` in `production.py` after `configure_logging` (routes through Python logging's StreamHandler.flush() per-record). Also fixed a latent persist bug: `persist_lightgbm_v1` hardcoded `PITCH_FEATURE_COLUMNS` (31) instead of `bundle.feature_cols`, would have crashed on post-head's 41 features. **Live `--model post --skip-cv` reproduces full bundle in 13:01 wall**: model.lgb 45 MB, calibrator.json, metadata.json + eval/{metrics.json, reliability_diagrams.png, confusion_matrix.png, feature_importance.csv}, training_data.parquet 24 MB. **Partial CV (3 of 4 folds, full run killed in fold 4 at the 30-min target boundary): fold 1 Brier=0.14788, fold 2 Brier=0.14778 (match pre exactly — pre-2024 has no Tier 4 signal), fold 3 Brier=0.10386 (~30% lower than pre — Tier 4 adds real signal once it lands in the test year). ECE < 0.02 on every fold.** Full --model post (all CV + production) projects to ~57 min wall on the Mac dev box; v1 bundle on disk is sufficient for 2b.3 to scaffold. **129 Python + 38 Java tests, 0 failures.** Next: 2b.3 (Spring serve for post head) — the persisted v1 bundle satisfies the upstream dependency.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Full feature pipeline with strict temporal cutoffs
Four leakage tests in CI (required-status, blocks merge)
Property tests for the leakage detector itself (Hypothesis-style)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Generate random temporally-shuffled datasets, assert the 4 leakage tests fire correctly on known-leaky AND known-clean inputs. Proves the leakage tests aren't fooling themselves. Without this, the leakage tests are theater.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Coverage floor 75% enforced in CI for training/
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Matches StudyForesight's bar. ruff + pytest-cov in CI, fail build under 75%. Coverage report uploaded as Actions artifact.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Contract tests on the API via Schemathesis (against Spring's /v3/api-docs)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Schemathesis is Python; it just needs the OpenAPI schema URL. Catches drift between the documented contract and the implementation. Runs as a separate CI job, required-to-merge.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Rolling-origin temporal CV harness (4 folds 2015–2025)
Pre-pitch head: trained, calibrated, registered, served at /predict/pitch
Post-pitch head: separate registry entry, served
Physics simulator (Nathan drag/Magnus ODE, RK4)
Physics validation: reproduce 100 known Statcast trajectories within tolerance
Multi-output MLP: shared backbone + 30 per-park heads + 30 isotonic calibrators
LR baseline registered for each role (no primary without baseline)
ECE < 0.02 on test data per model

mvp_cuts.cut: Drop post-pitch head, keep pre-pitch only

mvp_cuts.when: End of Wk 15 if 2c at risk
mvp_cuts.cut: Drop physics retrodiction, fall back to per-park naive subsets

phases.name: ML Systems Wrapper
phases.exit_criterion: Trigger retrain manually → new candidate registered with eval → promote through shadow → champion via API → traffic shifts visible in logs → old champion archived. Full lifecycle, end-to-end.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: 4-stage lifecycle (candidate / shadow / champion / archived)
Feature schema hashing enforced at registration (HARD FAIL on mismatch)
Shadow-mode default + real A/B via game_id Murmur3 bucketing
Async batched prediction logging to ClickHouse
Drift: PSI per feature, PSI on predictions, calibration on observed outcomes
Synthetic drift tests (inject known shifts, verify detection)
retraining_queue + three triggers (scheduled / drift / manual)
End-to-end retrain test passing
JMH benchmark suite for the inference path + perf-regression CI gate (25% threshold)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Benchmarks: ONNX session prediction p50/p99, A/B router decision time, calibrator apply time. ./gradlew benchmark in CI, scripts/check_benchmarks.py compares against the previous commit's numbers, fails on >25% regression.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Repository pattern formalized in data/ — 5 explicit aggregates
exit_criteria_checklist.details: ModelRegistryRepository, ExperimentResultsRepository, PredictionLogRepository, DriftMetricsRepository, RetrainingQueueRepository. Each owns its table(s) and exposes a typed API. No JdbcTemplate calls leak outside data/. Matches StudyForesight's pattern.

mvp_cuts.cut: Cut real A/B path; keep shadow only
Cut automated drift triggering; keep drift measurement, manual retrains

phases.notes: NEVER cut the registry — it's the spine

phases.name: Frontend Build-Out
phases.exit_criterion: All 5 pages exist, all have loading/error/empty states, Lighthouse > 80, bundle < 300KB gzipped initial

exit_criteria_additions.label: TS strict flags enabled: noUnusedLocals + noUnusedParameters + noImplicitReturns + noUncheckedIndexedAccess
exit_criteria_additions.details: Matches StudyForesight's tsconfig.app.json. Adds compile-time safety without runtime cost. Pay the migration cost early when the codebase is small.

exit_criteria_additions.label: Security headers on Vercel deployment: COOP, CORP, CSP frame-ancestors 'none'
exit_criteria_additions.details: Configured in vercel.json. Trivial 1-hour addition that matches StudyForesight's API security_headers.py posture for the frontend side.

mvp_cuts.cost: Lose live-watching demo; Park Explorer 'today's BIPs' partially compensates

mvp_cuts.cut: Cut visual ambition on About page

phases.notes: NEVER cut Ops Dashboard — it's the recruiter-clicked page

phases.name: Polish + Operate
phases.exit_criterion: Project publicly accessible, README links to all artifacts, ≥1 drift event observed and documented in postmortem, system running ≥4 weeks with documented uptime

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Polish pass across all pages (typography, spacing, color, motion, a11y)
One specific Park Explorer heatmap iteration ('fine' → 'memorable')
Performance optimizations (bundle audit, image opt, lazy loading)
README rewrite, design decisions doc, methodology page final content
Public launch: post to r/baseball, r/sabermetrics, r/programming, HN
Operate through rest of MLB season
Mid-season drift postmortem WRITTEN (the centerpiece artifact)
Nightly k6 load test gating on SLA (p99 prediction latency < 50ms, error rate < 0.1%)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: k6 against the deployed API, runs nightly via GitHub Actions cron, posts annotations to Grafana on each run. SLA matches design.md's stated latency target. Failure pings Discord.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: docs/runbooks/ROLLBACK.md — top-level runbook covering deploy / migration / model promotion / drift retrain rollbacks
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Pulls together per-skill rollback procedures (deploy-safely, promote-model, schema-migration-author) into one document an on-call you reads at 2am. Each scenario: detection signal → decision criteria → exact commands → verification steps.

phases.mvp_cuts: Extend timeline rather than cut. Polish compounds.
phases.notes: Exception: if dragging into October, accept 'ships in winter, postmortem in 2027 spring training' as the actual plan rather than panic-cutting.

phases.name: Mid-Season Hardening Sweep
phases.weeks: concurrent with Phase 5, ~2 weeks of dedicated time mid- or end-of-season
phases.exit_criterion: A single docs/hardening/{date}_sweep.md exists with an Impact table of ≥8 items, each with before/after metrics and a file/PR reference. The table is referenced in the README and the Phase 6 hiring-readiness work.
phases._purpose: This is the artifact that specifically maps to StudyForesight's Q2 2026 hardening sweep. It is what makes an L5 reviewer screenshot the README. Without this, the 'I operated this through a season' story is incomplete — operation without follow-through is just deployment.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: docs/hardening/observations.md kept up during operation — running list of anything that surprised you (slow queries, perf bottlenecks, observability gaps, security tightening opportunities, runbook friction, where discipline rules saved or failed you)
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Low-friction during operations: just append observations as they happen. The triage step at sweep time selects the top 8–15 by impact-to-effort.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Triage at sweep start: top 8–15 items by impact-to-effort selected from observations.md
Each selected item implemented with a measurable before/after
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Before/after must be a real metric or evidence, not 'feels better.' Examples: query latency p99, test count, RLS coverage count, false-positive rate on drift detector, time-to-rollback in drill.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Impact table written in docs/hardening/{date}_sweep.md
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Format: | Area | Before | After | Where |. Mirror StudyForesight's Q2 sweep table layout exactly — it's a known-good pattern that reads well.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: ADRs updated where decisions were revised during the sweep
Sweep doc referenced from README and Phase 6 hiring-readiness work

phases.mvp_cuts: Do not cut. If energy limited, SHRINK the number of items in the table — but produce the document. Even 5 honest hardening items with before/after metrics is better than nothing.
phases.notes: Can run concurrent with Phase 5's operate-through-season work. The artifact is the deliverable, not the calendar week. Pair with the drift postmortem (Phase 5) and the README (Phase 6) — they are a matched set.

phases.name: Hiring Readiness
phases.exit_criterion: A hiring manager visiting the GitHub repo in 60 seconds gets the right impression and can find the postmortem, the Ops Dashboard, and proof of collaboration without scrolling. The project is interview-ready as a 90-second verbal walkthrough.
phases._purpose: Phase 5 ships the engineering. Phase 6 makes the engineering legible to a hiring audience. Different work, separate time block. Do not skip these — they're the difference between a project an HM bookmarks and one they close the tab on.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: README is the interview opener — one-paragraph origin story, screenshot of Ops Dashboard, link to drift postmortem, tech decisions with rationale, live link to dashboard
exit_criteria_checklist.details: First 30 seconds of HM eyeball-time IS the README. Spend disproportionate time here. Lead with what-it-is + why-interesting in one sentence each. The Ops Dashboard screenshot should be above the fold. Tech-decisions section pulls highlights from docs/decisions.md with one-line rationales — do not link to the raw log, summarize.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: At least one merged OSS PR in a project adjacent to The Bullpen's stack
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Goal: one defensible answer to 'tell me about working with a maintainer through code review.' Doesn't need to be big — a docs fix or a real bug you hit during Phase 2/3 counts. Targets in priority order: onnxruntime (Java bindings), clickhouse-java / clickhouse-jdbc, lightgbm, pybaseball, mantine, tanstack/query, taste-skill, frontend-design. Only file a PR if you genuinely hit something — manufactured contributions read worse than no contribution.

exit_criteria_checklist.label: Drift postmortem written in real SRE format (Five Whys, timeline, threshold analysis, runbook updates), not blog format
exit_criteria_checklist.details: Steal the structure from real public postmortems — Cloudflare, GitHub, Stripe, Fly.io all publish theirs. Sections: TL;DR (3 lines), Timeline (detection → mitigation → resolution with timestamps), Root Cause (Five Whys), Impact (what was wrong with predictions for how long), What Went Well / What Went Poorly, Lessons Learned, Action Items (with owners and dates — even if owner is always 'me'), Runbook Updates. If the season is too quiet to produce a natural drift event, INDUCE one in a controlled way (inject a known shift, watch detection fire, write up the response) and document the synthetic nature honestly.

supplementary_artifacts.label: Lessons-learned doc separate from the postmortem — broader, covers the whole season
supplementary_artifacts.details: What you'd architect differently. What surprised you. What discipline rules paid off vs. felt like overhead. What you'd tell yourself if starting again. Demonstrates reflection — the trait that separates 'junior who shipped' from 'junior who'll grow fast'.

supplementary_artifacts.label: 60-second verbal pitch rehearsed
supplementary_artifacts.details: The baseball-origin → ML-systems-framing → self-hosted-for-failure-modes → target-roles paragraph. Practice it spoken, not just written. If it feels stilted, rewrite. HMs can tell a memorized script from a story you actually believe.

supplementary_artifacts.label: Ops Dashboard kept accessible post-launch, populated with realistic data even after the season ends
supplementary_artifacts.details: Recruiters will visit months later. A dead dashboard is a worse signal than no dashboard. Either keep ingest running on a low cadence in the off-season, or freeze the dashboard at a known-good state with a banner noting the snapshot date.

phases.mvp_cuts: Do not cut. This phase IS the resume work; cutting it wastes Phases 0–5.
phases.notes: Phase 6 is unusual in that it can run concurrent with or after Phase 5 — write the README during the season, file the OSS PR whenever you hit a real bug, draft the postmortem as drift events happen. The 30–50h estimate assumes deliberate dedicated time even if some of it overlaps with Phase 5 calendar weeks.

soft_cut_priority: 1. Drop post-pitch head, keep pre-pitch only (~20h)
2. Drop A/B real-routing, keep shadow only (~10h)
3. Drop automated drift retraining, keep manual (~5h)
5. Drop physics retrodiction, fall back to per-park naive subsets (~25h, weakens model)
